entries.objective: Research production-viable tech stack for 3D math learning experience (Roblox-like, elementary school, Vercel deployment)
entries.rationale: Decomposed into 7 research questions across 4 domains: technology (Q1,Q2,Q6), deployment (Q3), governance (Q4), architecture (Q5,Q7)

decisions.Q1: external — tier-1 official docs for three.js, babylon.js, playcanvas
decisions.Q2: external — tier-1 official docs for colyseus, partykit, liveblocks

requirements.description: Production-viable 3D rendering in browser for ages 6-12
Deployable on Vercel with acceptable performance

questions.text: What 3D rendering engines are production-viable for browser-based educational apps in 2026?
questions.scope: WebGL/WebGPU engines with React integration, 2025-2026 releases
questions.successCriteria: >= 3 candidates compared on performance, ecosystem, React support

questions.text: What multiplayer frameworks support real-time educational 3D collaboration?
questions.successCriteria: >= 2 candidates compared on latency, hosting, Vercel compat

questions.text: What are Vercel's deployment constraints for 3D web applications?
questions.scope: Function limits, CDN, edge runtime, WebSocket support
questions.successCriteria: Concrete limits documented: regions, concurrency, /tmp, streaming

questions.text: What COPPA/child safety requirements apply to ages 6-12 educational platforms?
questions.scope: US COPPA, EU Age-Appropriate Design Code

questions.text: How does ontology-driven architecture map to educational domain entities?
questions.scope: D/L/A classification: Student, Lesson, Problem, Score, Progress
questions.successCriteria: Complete D/L/A mapping using BROWSE.md recipes

questions.text: What is WebGPU browser support status on target devices (school Chromebooks)?
questions.scope: Chrome, Safari, Firefox support matrix, fallback story
questions.successCriteria: Support matrix with dates + WebGL fallback viability

questions.text: What educational UX patterns maximize engagement for elementary math?
